title: scrophularia alata var. cinerea
intro: Scrophularia alata var. cinerea, also known as the gray-winged figwort, is a perennial plant species in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) characterized by its unique grayish-brown leaves and white, bell-shaped flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Scrophularia alata var. cinerea are believed to include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and improving digestion, while also exhibiting antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or infused oil for medicinal use, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb require further scientific research and verification.
